en.extract:
The rufous-throated honeyeater (Conopophila rufogularis) is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae.
It is endemic to northern Australia.
Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical mangrove forests, but it also functions well in urban environments due to the lack of nest predators.

The '''rufous-throated honeyeater''' ('''''Conopophila rufogularis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Meliphagidae]].
It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to northern [[Australia]].

Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical mangrove [[forest]]s, but it also functions well in urban environments due to the lack of nest predators.<ref>{{Cite thesis |title=Nest ecology of a threatened woodland avifauna |publisher=University of Queensland Library |first=Graham |last=Fulton |date=2020 |doi=10.14264/uql.2020.965 }}</ref>
